title: uragoga subtomentosa
intro: Uragoga subtomentosa, also known as the Uva Ursi or Bearberry, is a low-growing evergreen shrub native to North America. The medicinal properties of Uragoga subtomentosa lie in its leaves, which contain glycosides that have antiseptic and astringent properties, making it effective in treating urinary tract infections and other issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, prevent kidney stones, and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, all due to its antimicrobial properties. Uragoga subtomentosa can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or extract, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote urinary health and prevent infections.
